suppress.notes: CVE-2026-54513 affects jackson-databind's BasicPolymorphicTypeValidator.Builder.allowIfSubTypeIsArray(). This vulnerable jackson-databind version is only pulled in transitively (via org.wildfly.security:wildfly-elytron-jose-util -> wildfly-elytron-http-oidc) into the providedCompile and providedRuntime configurations, which are compile-only scope against APIs supplied by WildFly at deploy time and are not packaged into the deployable artifact. Neither this codebase nor the WildFly Elytron libraries that pull in this version call allowIfSubTypeIsArray() or use polymorphic type validation at all (verified by searching the compiled classes of wildfly-elytron-jose-util, wildfly-elytron-jose-jwk, wildfly-elytron-http-oidc and wildfly-elytron-auth), so the vulnerable code path is unreachable. The actual application runtime and compile classpaths already resolve jackson-databind to the fixed 2.22.1 version via the jackson-bom pin in gradle/libs.versions.toml.

CVE-2026-41851 is a DoS caused by unbounded cache growth in Spring Expression Language (SpEL) evaluation, specifically in the org.springframework.expression.spel package shipped in the spring-expression artifact. spring-core and spring-beans are only pulled in transitively via org.flowable:flowable-engine, which uses them for utility classes (e.g. BeanUtils, ClassUtils), not for SpEL evaluation. Flowable evaluates its own `${...}` expressions in BPMN/CMMN definitions using its own JUEL-based expression engine, not Spring's SpelExpressionParser. The spring-expression artifact that contains the vulnerable class is not a dependency of this project at all (confirmed absent from both the compileClasspath and runtimeClasspath configurations), so the vulnerable code path is unreachable regardless of the spring-core/spring-beans version present.

CVE-2026-41850 (SpEL algorithmic complexity DoS), CVE-2026-41848 (AntPathMatcher ReDoS), CVE-2026-41842 (Spring MVC/WebFlux static resource DoS) and CVE-2026-41838 (predictable spring-websocket session IDs) all require a Spring module that is not a dependency of this project: spring-expression, spring-webmvc, spring-webflux and spring-websocket respectively are all absent from both the compileClasspath and runtimeClasspath configurations. spring-core and spring-beans are only pulled in transitively via org.flowable:flowable-engine. Searching the compiled classes of every org.flowable jar (flowable-engine, flowable-engine-common, and all other flowable-* modules on the classpath) for any reference to org/springframework/* returns no matches at all, so Flowable does not call into AntPathMatcher or any other spring-core/spring-beans class either. The vulnerable code is therefore unreachable for all four CVEs.

CVE-2026-54428 (HTTP/2 HPACK decoder DoS) and CVE-2026-54399 (HTTP/1.1 message parser DoS) both affect Apache HttpComponents Core 5.x (5.4.2 and earlier / 5.5-beta1 and earlier), i.e. the org.apache.hc.core5 codebase that ships in the org.apache.httpcomponents.core5:httpcore5 / httpcore5-h2 artifacts. This project depends on the legacy, unrelated 4.x line (org.apache.httpcomponents:httpcore, package org.apache.http.*), pulled in transitively via httpclient:4.5.14 <- org.apache.solr:solr-solrj. The 4.x codebase was rewritten from scratch for 5.x and does not implement HTTP/2 at all, so it cannot contain the vulnerable HPACK decoder, and its HTTP/1.1 parser is different code than the one fixed upstream. httpcore5 / httpclient5 are not dependencies of this project. This is a known NVD/CPE name-collision false positive between the 4.x and 5.x httpcore artifact families.

CVE-2026-5795 is a broken access control / privilege escalation issue caused by JASPIAuthenticator leaving ThreadLocal values set across requests. That class lives in Jetty's server-side jetty-security / jetty-jaspi module. This project only depends on Jetty's client-side modules (jetty-client, jetty-io, jetty-http, jetty-util, jetty-alpn-client, jetty-alpn-java-client, http2-client, http2-common, http2-http-client-transport), pulled in transitively via org.apache.solr:solr-solrj as its HTTP/2 client transport. Confirmed there is no jetty-server, jetty-security or jetty-jaspi module anywhere in the dependency graph, so the vulnerable class is not present at all.

CVE-2025-11143 (differential URI parsing security bypass) and CVE-2026-6790 (request authority / Host header mismatch not enforced) both describe attack scenarios where an untrusted, externally supplied URI or Host header is interpreted differently by Jetty than by another component in the request path (a WAF, reverse proxy, or the server generating a redirect) - i.e. they are server-side request-handling concerns. This project only uses Jetty's client-side modules (jetty-client, jetty-http, jetty-io, jetty-util, jetty-alpn-client, jetty-alpn-java-client, http2-client, http2-common, http2-http-client-transport), pulled in transitively via org.apache.solr:solr-solrj as its HTTP/2 client transport, to make outbound requests to our own configured Solr endpoint. There is no jetty-server in the dependency graph, no reverse proxy/WAF pair whose differing interpretation of the same URI could be exploited, and the target URI/Host header pair used for these outbound requests is not attacker-controlled. The described bypass scenarios do not apply to an outbound-only HTTP client use case.

CVE-2024-9329 is an open redirect in the Eclipse GlassFish *application server*'s admin console, via the Host header on its '/management/domain' management REST endpoint. relaxng-datatype and rngom are unrelated XML RELAX NG schema datatype libraries, part of the Eclipse/GlassFish *JAXB* project (org.glassfish.jaxb), pulled in transitively via org.jboss.resteasy:resteasy-jaxb-provider -> resteasy-multipart-provider. This application is deployed on WildFly, not GlassFish, does not embed the GlassFish application server or its admin console, and has no '/management/domain' endpoint. This is an NVD/CPE name-collision false positive between the unrelated "GlassFish" application server product and the "GlassFish JAXB" reference implementation project that happen to share the glassfish name.

CVE-2026-0871 is an improper access control flaw in Keycloak's handling of "unmanaged" user profile attributes. Per the GitHub Security Advisory, the actually affected Maven package is org.keycloak:keycloak-server-spi-private (< 26.5.2) - a Keycloak *server*-side SPI module. keycloak-admin-client and keycloak-client-common-synced are unrelated client SDK artifacts used by this application only to call the Keycloak Admin REST API of an externally deployed Keycloak server; they do not contain keycloak-server-spi-private or the vulnerable authorization logic. This repository does not build or embed the Keycloak server itself, so this CVE is not fixable from this codebase - the actual Keycloak server instance this application connects to should be kept at >= 26.5.2 independently.

The following CVEs all require a Spring module that is not a dependency of this project: - CVE-2026-22737 (script template view path traversal), CVE-2026-22740 (WebFlux multipart temp file leak), CVE-2026-22745 (MVC/WebFlux static resource DoS on Windows), CVE-2026-41839 (WebFlux session fixation), CVE-2026-41840 (WebFlux multipart memory leak), CVE-2026-41841 (MVC/WebFlux static resource info disclosure), CVE-2026-41843 (MVC/WebFlux static resource path traversal), CVE-2026-41844 (MVC/WebFlux open redirect via "/**" mapping) and CVE-2026-41853 (MVC/WebFlux multipart request smuggling) all require spring-webmvc and/or spring-webflux. - CVE-2026-41845 (JavaScriptUtils XSS) and CVE-2026-41854 (UriComponentsBuilder SSRF) both require spring-web (org.springframework.web.util package). - CVE-2026-41846 (JSP form tag XSS) requires the spring-webmvc JSP tag library. spring-webmvc, spring-webflux and spring-web are all absent from both the compileClasspath and runtimeClasspath configurations - this application is a Jakarta EE/JAX-RS app on WildFly and does not use Spring MVC/WebFlux at all. spring-core and spring-beans are only pulled in transitively via org.flowable:flowable-engine, which (as established for CVE-2026-41851/41850/41848/41842/41838 above) does not call into any org.springframework class.

CVE-2026-41852 is a Spring Expression Language (SpEL) authorization bypass allowing arbitrary zero-argument method invocation, in the org.springframework.expression.spel package shipped in the spring-expression artifact. As established above for CVE-2026-41851/41850, spring-expression is not a dependency of this project at all, and Flowable (the only consumer of spring-core/spring-beans) does not use Spring's SpEL evaluator, so this code path is unreachable.

CVE-2026-54514 (SSRF via eager DNS resolution in JDKFromStringDeserializer), CVE-2026-54515 and CVE-2026-54516 (JsonIgnore/JsonProperty exclusion bypass), and CVE-2026-54517 and CVE-2026-54518 (@JsonView filter bypass) all affect jackson-databind versions that include 2.21.1, fixed in 2.18.8/ 2.18.9, 2.21.4/2.21.5 and 3.1.4 depending on the specific CVE. As established for CVE-2026-54513 above, this jackson-databind 2.21.1 is only pulled in transitively (via org.wildfly.security:wildfly-elytron-jose-util -> wildfly-elytron-http-oidc) into the providedCompile and providedRuntime configurations - compile-only scope against APIs supplied by WildFly at deploy time, not packaged into the deployable artifact. The application's actual runtime and compile classpaths resolve jackson-databind to 2.22.1 via the jackson-bom pin in gradle/libs.versions.toml, which is already fixed for every one of these CVEs.
